segments.text: When you sit down to eat with a ruler,
Consider carefully what is before you
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: And put a knife to your throat if you are a man given to appetite.
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: Do not desire his delicacies, for they are deceptive food.
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: Do not overwork to be rich; Because of your own understanding, cease!
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: Will you set your eyes on that which is not? For riches certainly make themselves wings; They fly away like an eagle toward heaven.
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: Do not eat the bread of a miser, Nor desire his delicacies;
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: For as he thinks in his heart, so is he.
Eat and drink!
segments.speaker: Miser

segments.text: But his heart is not with you.
The morsel you have eaten, you will vomit up, And waste your pleasant words.
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: Do not speak in the hearing of a fool, For he will despise the wisdom of your words.
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: Do not remove the ancient landmark, Nor enter the fields of the fatherless;
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: For their Redeemer is mighty; He will plead their cause against you.
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: Apply your heart to instruction, And your ears to words of knowledge.
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: Do not withhold correction from a child, For if you beat him with a rod, he will not die.
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: You shall beat him with a rod, And deliver his soul from hell.
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: My son, if your heart is wise, My heart will rejoice—indeed, I myself;
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: Yes, my inmost being will rejoice When your lips speak right things.
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: Do not let your heart envy sinners, But be zealous for the fear of the Lord all the day;
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: For surely there is a hereafter, And your hope will not be cut off.
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: Hear, my son, and be wise; And guide your heart in the way.
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: Do not mix with winebibbers, Or with gluttonous eaters of meat;
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: For the drunkard and the glutton will come to poverty, And drowsiness will clothe a man with rags.
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: Listen to your father who begot you, And do not despise your mother when she is old.
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: Buy the truth, and do not sell it, Also wisdom and instruction and understanding.
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: The father of the righteous will greatly rejoice, And he who begets a wise child will delight in him.
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: Let your father and your mother be glad, And let her who bore you rejoice.
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: My son, give me your heart, And let your eyes observe my ways.
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: For a harlot is a deep pit, And a seductress is a narrow well.
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: She also lies in wait as for a victim, And increases the unfaithful among men.
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: Who has woe? Who has sorrow? Who has contentions? Who has complaints? Who has wounds without cause? Who has redness of eyes?
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: Those who linger long at the wine, Those who go in search of mixed wine.
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: Do not look on the wine when it is red, When it sparkles in the cup, When it swirls around smoothly;
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: At the last it bites like a serpent, And stings like a viper.
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: Your eyes will see strange things, And your heart will utter perverse things.
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: Yes, you will be like one who lies down in the midst of the sea, Or like one who lies at the top of the mast,
saying:
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They have struck me, but I was not hurt; They have beaten me, but I did not feel it. When shall I awake, that I may seek another drink?
segments.speaker: Drunkard
